title: Indulgent Canadian Pecan Stew
description: A creamy, nutty stew that warms the soul with a maple‑infused pecan broth, finished in a single wok for effortless comfort. The silky texture and rich flavor make it a cozy dinner for chilly evenings.

ingredients.name: canadian butter
ingredients.notes: rendered to use as base oil

instructions: Heat the wok over medium heat and add the butter until it melts and foams.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about five minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, grated ginger, and a pinch of red pepper flakes, cooking until fragrant, about one minute.
Add the chopped pecans and toast lightly, stirring frequently, for two minutes.
Pour in the vegetable broth and coconut milk, then stir in maple syrup, tahini, soy sauce, salt, and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes, allowing flavors to meld.
Use a wooden spoon to mash some of the pecans against the side of the wok, thickening the stew to a velvety consistency.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed, adding more salt, pepper, or maple syrup to suit your preference.
Remove from heat, swirl in fresh cilantro if desired, and let the stew rest for two minutes before serving.
Plate the stew hot, garnishing with a few whole pecans and a drizzle of maple syrup.
